_doc: Ground-truth test cases for the impact-check service. Each case pairs an upstream change with a KNOWN downstream Mantle adaptation commit (human-verified). Used to validate that the service detects 'this upstream PR requires a Mantle adaptation'. See README.md for methodology. Uses the `--ref` flag on scripts/analyze-pr.ts (implemented) to pin the downstream clone to a specific commit.
_methodology: For each case: PIN the downstream target clone to `test.positive.pinRef` (the parent of the adaptation commit = pre-adaptation state) and run impact-check on the upstream PR -> EXPECT affected=yes (adaptation still needed). PIN to `test.negative.pinRef` (the adaptation commit itself) -> EXPECT affected=no (already adapted).
target_branch_note: Both adaptation commits live on mantle-xyz/mantle-v2 branch mantle-elysium. The downstream file op-service/sources/types.go (struct RPCHeader) is Mantle's OWN mirror of go-ethereum's core/types.Header, maintained in-repo (NOT via the external op-geth go.mod replace) — so it IS readable in the mantle-v2 clone, making these good in-clone positive tests.

cases.title: EIP-7843 adds Header.SlotNumber; Mantle mirrors it into RPCHeader

upstream.verified: CONFIRMED 2026-06-15: #33589 is a combined PR — it implements the SLOTNUM opcode (core/vm) AND introduces the core/types.Header.SlotNumber field in the SAME commit. Its diff to core/types/block.go adds `SlotNumber *uint64 `json:"slotNumber" rlp:"optional"`` (lines are pure additions; the parent had no such field), which is exactly the field the downstream RPCHeader mirrors. #33589/f811bfe4f is the correct upstream anchor; no separate companion PR exists or is needed.

groundTruthAdaptation.author: pandainzoo (gary.zhu@mantle.xyz)
groundTruthAdaptation.what: Adds `SlotNumber *uint64 `json:"slotNumber" rlp:"optional"`` to RPCHeader and passes hdr.SlotNumber through in CreateGethHeader().

positive.note: pre-adaptation parent — RPCHeader does NOT yet have SlotNumber

expected.rationale: Feature-parity / compatibility mirror sync so Mantle can deserialize headers carrying slotNumber. Field is rlp:optional and ignored in legacy headers, so a missing field would not halt the chain -> NOT critical. Per impact-alert-severity-bar this belongs in the digest, not a critical push.

cases.title: Pre-Amsterdam payloads must omit empty slotNumber; Mantle adds ,omitempty

upstream.verified: CONFIRMED 2026-06-15: #34704 IS the correct upstream despite its misleading 'testing_buildBlockV1' title. Its PR description's first item and its diff add `,omitempty` to SlotNumber on the engine payload types: `SlotNumber *hexutil.Uint64 `json:"slotNumber"`` -> `json:"slotNumber,omitempty"` (and the matching *uint64 struct), so pre-Amsterdam ExecutableData/ExecutionPayload no longer serialize a null slotNumber. This is the upstream analogue of the downstream `,omitempty` fix applied to RPCHeader. The earlier 'does NOT match' note judged by PR title only and was wrong.

groundTruthAdaptation.author: pandainzoo (gary.zhu@mantle.xyz)
groundTruthAdaptation.what: Changes `json:"slotNumber"` -> `json:"slotNumber,omitempty"` so pre-Amsterdam headers don't serialize `"slotNumber":null` and stay JSON-compatible with upstream/peers.

positive.note: pre-adaptation parent — SlotNumber present but WITHOUT ,omitempty

expected.rationale: REVISED after implementation: the upstream change is on ExecutableData (engine/payload domain). Per the cross-struct DOMAIN guardrail (§3 of the spec), a payload-domain change maps to a payload-domain mirror (op-service ExecutionPayload), NOT to the header-domain RPCHeader. ExecutionPayload deliberately does not carry slotNumber (verified at tip), so there is no synced downstream mirror to drift against -> no required adaptation. The human's RPCHeader omitempty was a parallel cosmetic decision the (domain-respecting) structural detector neither can nor should attribute to this PR. The original 'yes' expectation contradicted the spec's own domain guardrail; this case is NOT detectable by domain-respecting structural mirror detection. Kept as a documented negative/limitation case.

_validationResults_2026-06-15.note: Live replay after implementing the contract-drift detector (docs/spark/2026-06-15-...). Honest outcomes vs the locked `expected`:

C1-positive.detail: Pre-pass found RPCHeader (sibling-overlap 21 vs upstream Header) MISSING SlotNumber; deterministic stale-mirror override forced affected=yes with a contractCheck code_evidence citing the full RPCHeader struct. THE headline fix: this exact case was 'uncertain' before. Severity rated critical by the LLM (RLP hash path); confidence=medium so it routes to digest, no push.

C1-negative.detail: Mirror correctly detected as SYNCED (RPCHeader has SlotNumber) — no false positive. Verdict stayed uncertain (not 'no') due to residual EXTERNAL-dependency uncertainty (op-geth source not in clone), which is the documented out-of-scope limitation, not a mirror issue. Consistent with the pre-fix baseline; not a regression.

C2-positive.detail: Expected REVISED to 'no' (see case). CONTRADICTION FOUND IN SPEC: the locked C2-positive=yes assumed the upstream ExecutableData tag change maps to RPCHeader. But the spec's own cross-struct guardrail requires SAME semantic domain, and ExecutableData (engine/payload domain) maps structurally to op-service ExecutionPayload (payload domain), NOT to RPCHeader (header domain). Verified at tip: Mantle deliberately mirrors slotNumber ONLY in RPCHeader, never in ExecutionPayload. So #34704's ExecutableData tag change has no synced downstream mirror to drift against → no required adaptation. The human's RPCHeader omitempty was a parallel cosmetic decision a structural detector cannot (and per the domain guardrail, should not) attribute to #34704. C2 is not detectable by domain-respecting structural mirror detection.

_validationResults_2026-06-15.verdict: Core fix validated (C1-positive yes). Negatives produce no false positives. C2 reframed honestly per the spec's own domain guardrail.

_regressionDoc: Reproducible no-regression cases (replace the ephemeral DB-row IDs #99/#100/#103/#95). Each pins a stable ref and asserts the contract-drift mirror phase did NOT change the prior depends_on verdict. Run via scripts/analyze-pr.ts with --ref. mantle-elysium ref pinned to a 2026-06-15 commit for repeatability.

regressionCases.rationale: Pure version-number bump, no source change; revm consumed via [patch] at a pinned rev; NO local mirror of any changed contract. Mirror phase must find nothing and fall back to the manifest/consumer verdict (no). Guards against the mirror phase inventing a false yes.

Changed reth crates are consumed as git deps (source not in clone) and there is NO local mirror of the changed API. Must stay uncertain (safe, no false alert) — the mirror phase must NOT turn this into a yes just because an identifier coincidentally appears somewhere.
